ucs.title: [LLM] LazyScripter (G0140) — open-source toolkit retrieval from GitHub raw / paste-site staging via LOLBin script interpreters
ucs.description: Hunts the signature LazyScripter staging behaviour: a Windows script-interpreter LOLBin (mshta, powershell, wscript/cscript, bitsadmin, certutil, curl, rundll32, regsvr32) calling raw.githubusercontent.com / pastebin / paste.ee / anonfiles to pull the next stage of an open-source RAT bundle (Koadic, Octopus, LuminosityLink, RMS, KOADIC, Quasar, njRat). Malwarebytes (Feb 2021) and follow-up CrowdStrike/MSFT reporting both pin GitHub-raw-hosted toolkits as the through-line for this airline-targeting actor — almost no other named cluster runs droppers that hard-code raw.githubusercontent.com URLs
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as cmdline values(Processes.parent_process_name) as parent values(Processes.parent_process) as parent_cmdline from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe") AND (Processes.process="*raw.githubusercontent.com*" OR Processes.process="*gist.githubusercontent.com*" OR Processes.process="*pastebin.com*" OR Processes.process="*paste.ee*" OR Processes.process="*anonfiles.com*" OR Processes.process="*transfer.sh*" OR Processes.process="*controlc.com*" OR Processes.process="*cdn.discordapp.com*") AND NOT (Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE")) by host Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.dest Processes.process_id Processes.parent_process_id
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| `security_content_ctime(firstTime)`
| `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
| where count < 25
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
// LazyScripter (G0140) — open-source RAT staging hosts
let _StagingHosts = dynamic([
    "raw.githubusercontent.com","gist.githubusercontent.com",
    "pastebin.com","paste.ee","anonfiles.com",
    "transfer.sh","controlc.com","cdn.discordapp.com"]);
let _LOLBins = dynamic([
    "mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe",
    "bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe"]);
// (1) cmdline-embedded URL — the canonical `mshta http://raw.githubusercontent.com/...` shape
let ProcessHits =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where FileName in~ (_LOLBins)
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_StagingHosts)
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ParentCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
              Child = FileName,
              ChildCmd = ProcessCommandLine,
              SHA256, Source = "ProcessCmd";
// (2) actual outbound socket from the LOLBin to the staging host
let NetworkHits =
    DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_LOLBins)
    | where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
    | where RemoteUrl has_any (_StagingHosts)
    | where RemoteIPType == "Public"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName,
              AccountName = InitiatingProcessAccountName,
              Parent = InitiatingProcessParentFileName,
              ParentCmd = "",
              Child = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              ChildCmd = strcat(InitiatingProcessCommandLine, " -> ", RemoteUrl),
              SHA256 = InitiatingProcessSHA256,
              Source = "NetEgress";
union ProcessHits, NetworkHits
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _StagingHosts = dynamic([
    "raw.githubusercontent.com","gist.githubusercontent.com",
    "pastebin.com","paste.ee","anonfiles.com",
    "transfer.sh","controlc.com","cdn.discordapp.com"]);
let _LOLBins = dynamic([
    "mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe",
    "bitsadmin.exe","curl.exe","certutil.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","cmd.exe"]);
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where EventID == 4688
| where Account !endswith "$"
| extend BinName = tolower(tostring(split(NewProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where BinName in (_LOLBins)
| where CommandLine has_any (_StagingHosts)
| project TimeGenerated, Computer, Account, NewProcessName, CommandLine,
          ParentProcessName, TokenElevationType
| order by TimeGenerated desc
ucs.sigma_yaml:
title: LazyScripter (G0140) LOLBin Retrieval From GitHub/Paste-Site Staging
id: 7b3a91c4-1cab-44e0-9d4f-lazyscript01
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects the signature LazyScripter (G0140 / Malwarebytes Feb-2021) staging
  technique: Windows script-interpreter LOLBins fetching open-source RAT
  toolkits (Koadic, Octopus, LuminosityLink, RMS, Quasar, njRat) from
  raw.githubusercontent.com / gist / pastebin / paste.ee / anonfiles. The
  combination of script LOLBin + GitHub-raw URL on cmdline is rare in legit
  enterprise software but a hard-coded pattern in LazyScripter droppers.
references:
  - https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0140/
  - https://www.malwarebytes.com/blog/news/2021/02/lazyscripter-from-empire-to-double-rat
author: Detection Engineering
date: 2026/05/03
tags:
  - attack.t1102
  - attack.t1105
  - attack.t1218.005
  - attack.t1059.001
  - attack.t1059.005
  - attack.g0140
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_image:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\mshta.exe'
      - '\powershell.exe'
      - '\pwsh.exe'
      - '\wscript.exe'
      - '\cscript.exe'
      - '\bitsadmin.exe'
      - '\curl.exe'
      - '\certutil.exe'
      - '\rundll32.exe'
      - '\regsvr32.exe'
      - '\cmd.exe'
  selection_url:
    CommandLine|contains:
      - 'raw.githubusercontent.com'
      - 'gist.githubusercontent.com'
      - 'pastebin.com'
      - 'paste.ee'
      - 'anonfiles.com'
      - 'transfer.sh'
      - 'controlc.com'
      - 'cdn.discordapp.com/attachments'
  filter_machine_acct:
    User|endswith: '$'
  condition: selection_image and selection_url and not filter_machine_acct
falsepositives:
  - Developer machines pulling boilerplate from public gists
  - DevOps bootstrap scripts (rare on user endpoints; baseline + allow-list)
level: high
ucs.rationale: LazyScripter is the rare named cluster whose tradecraft *is* GitHub-raw staging — every Malwarebytes/MSFT writeup of G0140 highlights raw.githubusercontent.com URLs hard-coded inside their .bat/.vbs/.lnk droppers calling mshta or powershell. Generic 'LOLBin downloads from internet' rules are noisy; pinning the destination to a small set of public-paste/code-hosting domains AND requiring the actor'
_sigma_issues: sigma parse error: Sigma rule identifier must be an UUID

ucs.title: [LLM] LazyScripter (G0140) — IATA/airline-themed lure script dropper chain to Run-key persistence (≤15 min)
ucs.description: Correlates the second LazyScripter signature: an Office/archive-utility/explorer.exe parent spawning wscript/cscript/mshta/powershell against a file in an Outlook attachment / Downloads / %TEMP% path (.vbs/.js/.hta/.bat/.lnk/.wsf), followed within 15 minutes on the same host by a HKCU\…\Run or RunOnce value being set by another script LOLBin. This is the canonical LazyScripter drop-and-pin chain — zipped IATA/visa/airline-job lures → embedded VBS/HTA → Run-key persistence for their Octopus/KOADIC stagers — and the parent + persistence pair is what makes it actor-specific rather than generic 'w
ucs.splunk:
| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as DropperTime values(Processes.process) as DropperCmd values(Processes.parent_process_name) as DropperParent values(Processes.process_name) as Dropper from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name IN ("explorer.exe","outlook.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe") AND Processes.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe") AND (Processes.process="*\\Outlook\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Content.Outlook\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\Downloads\\*" OR Processes.process="*\\AppData\\Local\\Temp\\*" OR Processes.process="*.vbs*" OR Processes.process="*.js*" OR Processes.process="*.hta*" OR Processes.process="*.bat*" OR Processes.process="*.lnk*" OR Processes.process="*.wsf*") AND NOT Processes.user IN ("*$","SYSTEM","LOCAL SERVICE","NETWORK SERVICE") by host Processes.user
| `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)`
| rename host as dest
| join type=inner dest [
    | tstats summariesonly=true min(_time) as PersistenceTime values(Registry.registry_path) as RegPath values(Registry.registry_value_name) as RegValueName values(Registry.registry_value_data) as RegValueData values(Registry.process_name) as RegWriter from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where (Registry.registry_path="*\\CurrentVersion\\Run\\*" OR Registry.registry_path="*\\CurrentVersion\\RunOnce\\*") AND Registry.action="modified" AND Registry.process_name IN ("wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe") by host
    | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)`
    | rename host as dest
]
| where PersistenceTime >= DropperTime AND PersistenceTime <= DropperTime + 900
| eval DropperTime=strftime(DropperTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S"), PersistenceTime=strftime(PersistenceTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| table DropperTime, PersistenceTime, dest, user, DropperParent, Dropper, DropperCmd, RegWriter, RegPath, RegValueName, RegValueData
ucs.kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _DropperBins  = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe"]);
let _LureParents  = dynamic(["explorer.exe","outlook.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe"]);
let _LurePaths    = dynamic([@"\Outlook\", @"\Content.Outlook\", @"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Temp\"]);
let _ScriptExts   = @"(?i)\.(vbs|js|jse|hta|bat|cmd|lnk|wsf|wsh|ps1)\b";
// (1) Lure-attachment script execution
let Droppers =
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where AccountName !endswith "$"
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_LureParents)
    | where FileName in~ (_DropperBins)
    | where ProcessCommandLine has_any (_LurePaths)
         or ProcessCommandLine matches regex _ScriptExts
    | project DropperTime = Timestamp, DeviceId, DeviceName, AccountName,
              DropperParent = InitiatingProcessFileName,
              Dropper       = FileName,
              DropperCmd    = ProcessCommandLine,
              DropperSHA256 = SHA256;
// (2) Run / RunOnce key written by a script LOLBin within 15 minutes
DeviceRegistryEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(LookbackDays)
| where ActionType in ("RegistryValueSet","RegistryKeyCreated")
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
| where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ (_DropperBins)
| where InitiatingProcessAccountName !endswith "$"
| join kind=inner Droppers on DeviceId
| where Timestamp between (DropperTime .. DropperTime + 15m)
| project DropperTime, PersistenceTime = Timestamp,
          DeviceName, AccountName,
          DropperParent, Dropper, DropperCmd, DropperSHA256,
          PersistWriter = InitiatingProcessFileName,
          PersistWriterCmd = InitiatingProcessCommandLine,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValueName, RegistryValueData
| order by DropperTime desc
ucs.sentinel_kql:
let LookbackDays = 14d;
let _DropperBins  = dynamic(["wscript.exe","cscript.exe","mshta.exe","powershell.exe","pwsh.exe","cmd.exe"]);
let _LureParents  = dynamic(["explorer.exe","outlook.exe","winrar.exe","7zfm.exe","winword.exe","excel.exe","powerpnt.exe"]);
let _LurePaths    = dynamic([@"\Outlook\", @"\Content.Outlook\", @"\Downloads\", @"\AppData\Local\Temp\", @"\Temp\"]);
let _ScriptExts   = @"(?i)\.(vbs|js|jse|hta|bat|cmd|lnk|wsf|wsh|ps1)\b";
let Droppers =
    ImProcessCreate
    | where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
    | where ActorUsername !endswith "$"
    | where ParentProcessName has_any (_LureParents)
    | extend ChildBin = tolower(tostring(split(TargetProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
    | where ChildBin in (_DropperBins)
    | where TargetProcessCommandLine has_any (_LurePaths)
         or TargetProcessCommandLine matches regex _ScriptExts
    | project DropperTime = TimeGenerated, DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
              DropperParent = ParentProcessName,
              Dropper       = ChildBin,
              DropperCmd    = TargetProcessCommandLine;
ImRegistryEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(LookbackDays)
| where RegistryKey has_any (@"\CurrentVersion\Run", @"\CurrentVersion\RunOnce")
| extend Writer = tolower(tostring(split(ActingProcessName, "\\")[-1]))
| where Writer in (_DropperBins)
| join kind=inner Droppers on $left.DvcHostname == $right.DvcHostname
| where TimeGenerated between (DropperTime .. DropperTime + 15m)
| project DropperTime, PersistenceTime = TimeGenerated,
          DvcHostname, ActorUsername,
          DropperParent, Dropper, DropperCmd,
          PersistWriter = Writer,
          RegistryKey, RegistryValue, RegistryValueData
| order by DropperTime desc
ucs.rationale: LazyScripter's airline-targeting tradecraft is unusually consistent: IATA/visa/job-application lure ZIPs containing a .vbs/.lnk/.hta dropper which Office/explorer/winrar opens, and the dropper drops a Run-key for the open-source RAT (KOADIC/Octopus/RMS) within seconds. Generic 'script from temp' or generic 'Run-key write' rules each generate hundreds of FPs/day; chaining the two with a 15-minute w
